questions.prompt: Simplify  3(2x − 4) + 5x.
Solve  2x + 7 = 19.
Factorise  x² + 7x + 12.
Expand and simplify  (x + 3)(x − 5).
Simplify  x⁵ × x³.
Evaluate  3² + 4².
Make x the subject of  y = 3x − 6.
Solve the simultaneous equations  x + y = 10  and  x − y = 4.
A straight line passes through (0, 2) and (3, 11). Find (a) its gradient and (b) its equation.
Solve  x² − 5x + 6 = 0.
The angles of a triangle are in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4. Find the three angles.
Find 15% of 240.
Simplify  (2x²y)³.

title: End of Topic Test 2 — Functions

questions.prompt: f(x) = 3x − 2.  Find f(4).
State the domain of  f(x) = 1/(x − 5).
f(x) = 2x + 1 and g(x) = x².  Find (f∘g)(x).
Find the inverse  f⁻¹(x)  for  f(x) = 4x − 3.
Describe fully the transformation that maps y = f(x) to y = f(x − 2) + 3.
(a) Solve  x² − 6x + 8 = 0.   (b) Find the discriminant of  x² + 2x + 5  and state the number of real roots.
For  f(x) = (2x + 1)/(x − 3),  write down (a) the vertical asymptote and (b) the horizontal asymptote.
The graph of y = f(x) is transformed to y = 2f(x) − 1. Describe the two transformations.

scheme: 8. vertical stretch scale factor 2 (A1)(A1); translation 1 down (A1)

questions.prompt: Convert  120°  to radians, giving an exact answer.
Write down the exact value of  sin 60°.
Find the exact value of  cos 150°.
Given sin θ = 3/5 and θ is acute, find cos θ.
A sector of a circle has radius 10 cm and angle 1.2 radians. Find (a) the arc length and (b) the area.
Solve  2 sin θ = √3  for  0° ≤ θ ≤ 360°.
In triangle ABC, a = 11 cm, b = 8 cm and C = 35°. Find (a) side c and (b) the area of the triangle.
An arithmetic sequence has u₁ = 5 and d = 3. Find (a) u₁₀ and (b) S₁₀.
Find the sum to infinity of  16 + 8 + 4 + …
Evaluate  8^(2/3).
Solve  log₂ x = 5.
f(x) = 2x + 1 and g(x) = x − 4. Find (f∘g)(x).
Find the exact value of  cos 120°.
The third term of a geometric sequence is 20 and the sixth term is 160. (a) Show r = 2.  (b) Find u₁.  (c) Find the sum of the first 10 terms.
Solve  x² − 6x + 8 = 0  and state the discriminant.
Solve  3ˣ = 50, giving your answer to 3 s.f.
In triangle ABC, A = 40°, B = 60° and a = 8 cm. Find side b.

title: End of Topic Test 4 — Statistics & Probability

questions.prompt: Find the mean of  5, 8, 8, 11, 13.
For the ordered data 3, 5, 7, 8, 12, 14, 18 find the interquartile range.
P(A) = 0.5, P(B) = 0.4 and P(A∩B) = 0.2. Find P(A∪B).
A box has 5 good and 3 faulty bulbs. Two are taken without replacement. Find (a) P(both good) and (b) P(exactly one faulty).
X ~ B(12, 0.25). Find (a) E(X) and (b) P(X = 3).
The masses of apples are N(120, 10²) g. Find (a) P(X < 130) and (b) the mass exceeded by 90% of apples.
Differentiate  y = 3x⁴ − 2x² + 7.
A curve has gradient function f′(x) = 2x − 4. Find the gradient at x = 3.
Find  ∫ (6x² + 2) dx.
For  y = x² − 4x:  (a) find dy/dx,  (b) find the equation of the tangent at x = 1.
For  y = x³ − 3x:  (a) find the x-coordinates of the stationary points,  (b) classify them using f″(x).
Evaluate  ∫₁⁴ (2x + 1) dx,  and hence state the area between y = 2x + 1, the x-axis and x = 1, x = 4.

title: End of Topic Test 6 — Internal Assessment Skills

questions.prompt: State what percentage of the final grade the Mathematics IA is worth.
A student proposes the IA title “Statistics.” Explain why this is unsuitable, then rewrite it as a focused, personal exploration question.
Name the five IA assessment criteria (A–E) and what each one rewards.
An IA uses correct mathematics but reads like a textbook with no personal voice. Which TWO criteria score poorly, and how would you improve each?
Explain the difference between description and reflection, giving one example of each.

scheme: 2. too broad / not a question / no personal context (A1)(A1); e.g. 'Is there a correlation between sleep and test scores in my class?' (A1)(A1)
3. A presentation, B mathematical communication, C personal engagement, D reflection, E use of mathematics (A1 each)
4. C (personal engagement) and D (reflection) (A1)(A1); C: add why chosen / own data; D: add critical comments on results & limitations (A1)(A1)
5. description states what happened; reflection interprets/questions it (A1)(A1); valid examples (A1)

questions.prompt: Find the sum of the first 10 terms of the arithmetic series 7 + 11 + 15 + …
Evaluate  log₃ 9 + log₃ 27.
Find the exact value of  sin 60°  and  cos 150°.
Differentiate  y = 2x³ − 5x + 4  and find the gradient at x = 1.
Find  ∫ (3x² − 4x + 5) dx.
A geometric series has first term 12 and sum to infinity 20. (a) Show r = 0.4.  (b) Find the sum of the first 4 terms.
Solve  4ˣ = 30  (3 s.f.), and solve  log₃(2x + 1) = 2.
For y = x³ − 3x, find the stationary points and classify them.
X ~ B(12, 0.25). Find E(X) and P(X = 3).
The mass of apples is N(120, 10²) g. Find P(X < 130) and the mass exceeded by 90% of apples.
